description_long: This is a vintage, early 20th-century monoplane with a fabric-covered, straight high wing and an open cockpit. The fuselage is a lightweight, exposed truss structure, primarily constructed from wood and metal, with a single propeller mounted at the front. The landing gear consists of two large spoked wheels at the front and a small tail skid at the rear. The tail assembly features a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The cockpit is open and centrally located, with a visible pilot figure seated behind the engine. The overall color scheme is a tan or beige fabric with metallic and dark accent components.
description: A vintage, fabric-covered monoplane with a high straight wing, open cockpit, exposed truss fuselage, and conventional tail. Features a front-mounted propeller and fixed landing gear with spoked wheels.
description_view_0: Side view showing the exposed truss fuselage, high wing, open cockpit, and large spoked wheels.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the full wingspan, open cockpit, and symmetrical tail surfaces.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, with clear visibility of the tail structure and landing gear.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the propeller, high wing configuration, and the open cockpit directly behind the engine.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing monoplane with straight, unswept wings and no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a rear tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Mid-fuselage, open single-seat cockpit located directly behind the engine with no canopy.
